meta.homeDescription: Portfolio of Layane Moura — fantasy maps, travel illustration, book covers and editorial work.
meta.contactDescription: Contact Layane Moura for illustration commissions, maps, book covers and collaborations.

site.description: Brazilian illustrator turning real and imaginary places into sensitive, organic narratives — fantasy maps, travel art, book covers and editorial work.

about.p1: Hi, I'm a curious illustrator, born in Brazil, but living around the world in a campervan for over a year now! I work with both digital and traditional media, like watercolor, most of the time mixing everything together.
about.p2: I'm passionate about new places, people and cultures, which definitely reflects in my work! And this goes for 'real worlds' as well as those that exist only in our imagination. So, I produce a lot of things in the universe of fantasy and ludic as well.
about.p3: My work allows applications in the most diverse types of media, mainly children's book covers, magazines, product design and advertising pieces.
about.p4: Why don't we keep in contact? :)

gallery.noProjects: No projects in this category yet.
gallery.seenAll: You've seen all {count} projects in this view.

items.text: Layane captured the spirit of our destination with a map that feels both informative and full of soul. Our travellers still share it on social media months later.
The fantasy map she created for my son's book is the heart of the story. Every corner hides a detail he discovers again and again — it's magical without being childish.
Working with Layane felt like collaborating with someone who truly listens. The cover illustration balances warmth and mystery exactly the way we imagined.
Her vintage travel posters gave our campaign a handcrafted, timeless quality. The line work and colour palette stood out immediately in a sea of stock imagery.
We needed a tourist map that felt inviting, not clinical. Layane delivered something our visitors treasure — clear, beautiful, and unmistakably Tuscan.

desmenia-treasure-map.description: Fantasy treasure map for the children's book Arthur, The Treasure Hunter — a world of sea monsters, forests, beaches and endless adventure.

simbionte-fantasy-map.description: Illustrated fantasy map for a narrative RPG universe — organic textures and cartographic storytelling.

meronte-fantasy-map.description: Contemporary fantasy atlas piece blending old-map references with a youthful, readable line.

where-i-have-been.description: Vintage-style travel posters and stickers celebrating places, memory and the joy of discovery.

toscana-travel-map.description: Tourist map of Tuscany with iconographic landmarks — digital and traditional media woven together.

lithgow-touristic-map.description: Destination map illustration with a soft vintage palette and clear, inviting geography.

south-africa-sticker.description: Commissioned sticker illustration for a travel agency — playful, collectible and full of place.

lets-be-friends-book-cover.description: Book cover illustration with narrative warmth — inviting, gentle and story-driven.

hands-of-time-poster.description: Editorial poster piece with contemplative mood and refined composition.

cowboy-camp-sticker.description: Whimsical sticker illustration with western charm and handcrafted line quality.

vila-pattern.description: Surface pattern inspired by place and rhythm — repeatable, organic and quietly poetic.
